---
title: "GitInfo"
date: 2026-06-25
author: G. Brückmann
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---


### Preparation: 
- some people need to install git, I am on a Mac, have XCODE installed, so it was already there

- in the correct working directory: initialise the git 
type in console: `git init` 


### Workflow is always: 
1) create an issue on GitHub (remember number of issue)
2) git pull to make local an online the same (blue arrow in r, or with code)
3) Change something to fix it (add/delete,...)
	*optional: and see the git status (type: `git status`)
4) make own branch for your changes (or select it) 
5) in console type `git add filename` or `git add .` (the point means everything) 
	* optional: and see the git status (type: `git status`)
6) in console: `git commit -m "Short note what you did"` commits the changes
	* optional: and see the git status (type: `git status`)
	
## Git Hub

If you are working with git hub and want to push it into the GitHub
5) in console type: `git push`

### Assume you created a repo in Github

1) copy its link (HTTPS or SSH, I use SSH) from "<>Code"  in green, "Local" on GitHub
2) write in console in a folder where you want the new link: ` git clone "the copied link"`
3) change working directory to the new link
	* maybe try `git status`


### Copy a remote from someone else and rename

#### Step-by-step

1. Wherever you want to have it, open the terminal and a new folder will be created with the clone in it
```shell
git clone https://git.sr.ht/~tpapastylianou/a-tidy-latex-project-template
```
2. Make the origin to the original-origin
```shell
git remote rename origin original-origin 
```

3. Create repository on GitHub and add the your github as new remote and set your branch to main
```shell
git remote add origin git@github.com:brueckmann/a-tidy-latex-project-template.git
git branch -M main
```

4. Push these changes to GitHub
```shell
git push -u origin main
```

#### Full step-by-step code: 
```shell
git clone https://git.sr.ht/~tpapastylianou/a-tidy-latex-project-template

git remote rename origin original-origin 

git remote add origin git@github.com:brueckmann/a-tidy-latex-project-template.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

### Closing an Issue in GitHub

Mention specific words in your `git commit -m "message"`, e.g.,  `git commit -m "closes #3 by doing xyz" ` 
[GitHub](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/using-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue#linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue-using-a-keyword)





## Gitignore

 A default for R projects: [https://github.com/github/gitignore/blob/main/R.gitignore](https://github.com/github/gitignore/blob/main/R.gitignore)
 

### When an ignored file is still around: 

1.  __Delete it.__  [Source](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/git-basics/ignoring-files)  
2. Remove it from git

```shell
git rm --cached FILENAME
```


## Git Error Handling

### Conflict resolution
```shell
$ git checkout --theirs -- path/to/conflicted-file.txt
```

You are good to repeat the process above :) 


### Solve error: Unable to create 'Folder/.git/index.lock': File exists.

```shell
# copy .git directory outside the project  
cp -r .git ../.git.bc  
  
# remove current .git  
rm -rf .git  
  
# remove `index.lock` on copied .git  
rm ../.git.bc/index.lock  
  
# move back .git  
mv ../.git.bc .git
```
[Source](https://medium.com/@rizqyhi/how-to-fix-git-error-fatal-unable-to-create-git-index-lock-file-exists-bad8302d283f)
